mechanism_narrative: A persistently low FEDFUNDS rate keeps policy supportive and lifts equity risk appetite, which should push the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days as markets price stronger growth and less immediate easing.
failure_condition: Reject if higher FEDFUNDS levels are followed by higher 20-day Treasury yield moves than lower FEDFUNDS levels, or if the sign is negative in more than 60% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises above a recession-like threshold, labor market weakness signals softer demand and slower inflation, which should drive the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations show a positive 20-day change in the 10-year yield more often than negative, or if the average 20-day yield change after trigger events is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates improving employment and retail demand, which should increase nominal growth expectations and lift the 10-year Treasury yield over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and RSAFS rises over the latest month
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive PAYEMS/RSAFS months do not outperform single-variable months in predicting higher 20-day yields, or if the conditional mean yield change is non-positive.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO after prior weakness signals production recovery and tighter resource utilization, which should lift the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days as growth and inflation expectations firm.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO is above its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO rebounds are followed by flat or negative 60-day yield changes, or if the sign flips negative in a majority of post-recovery windows.

mechanism_narrative: A housing upswing captured by HOUST rising above its recent trend implies stronger cyclical demand and future construction activity, which should push the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 5% versus its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-strength triggers are followed by negative or zero average 20-day yield changes, or if the effect disappears after one-year rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near zero and CPIAUCSL continues to run above its recent pace, the market should infer sustained policy accommodation and inflation pressure, raising the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and CPIAUCSL rises versus the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-rate/high-inflation condition does not yield positive average 20-day Treasury moves, or if the interaction term is not stronger than either single series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market deterioration regime, rising unemployment together with slowing payroll growth should dominate growth optimism and pull the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp while PAYEMS declines from the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if this combined labor-weakness signal does not produce negative 5-day yield changes on average, or if it fails more often than it succeeds after confirmed labor deterioration months.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales and industrial output both improve, the economy is signaling synchronized demand and production strength that should raise the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and INDPRO both rise versus their prior month
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS/INDPRO upswings fail to produce positive 60-day yield changes, or if the joint signal is weaker than either standalone proxy across rolling validation windows.
